name: Bourbon Pecan Pie-ny Times
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, cold and cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the crust by combining flour and salt in a large bowl.
2. Add the cold butter pieces and shortening to the flour mixture. Work quickly to mix until the dough resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add cold water, stirring with a fork until the dough holds together without being wet or sticky.
4. Divide the dough into two balls, wrap each in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. For the filling: beat eggs, then whisk together sugar, corn syrups, butter, bourbon, and salt until smooth.
7. Stir in chopped pecans.
8. Roll out one dough ball into a large circle on a lightly floured surface. Transfer to a 9-inch pie plate.
9. Press the crust into the plate, trimming any excess.
10. Pour the filling into the crust and spread evenly.
11. Place the second dough circle over the filling; seal and flute edges. Cut slits in the top.
12. Bake for 50-60 minutes until golden brown. Cool completely before serving.
